g.data-narration: Welcome to Module 4! Today we explore how smart contracts get deployed onto the blockchain.
g.text: How Smart Contracts Deploy onto the Blockchain

g.data-narration: A contract creation transaction is a special blockchain transaction that deploys new smart contract code.
g.text: What is a Contract Creation Transaction?
A special type of external transaction that deploys smart contracts.
Has NO "to" field (empty recipient) - signals deployment request.
Contains bytecode in data field; creates new contract account.

g.data-narration: This is the foundation for all decentralized applications - without deployment, no smart contracts exist.
g.text: Foundation of all DeFi and dApps
Regular TX: sends ETH to address

g.data-narration: A contract creation transaction has specific fields: from address, empty to field, bytecode data, optional value, and gas parameters.
The empty to field is the primary identifier - it signals to the network this is a deployment request, not a regular transfer.
g.text: The Critical To Field: MUST BE EMPTY
This empty field is what distinguishes deployment from regular transfers

g.data-narration: The data field contains the compiled creation bytecode - this is executed by the EVM to initialize and return the runtime code.
The deployment process has five key steps: initiation, execution, address generation, storage, and confirmation.
The process starts when an EOA signs and broadcasts the transaction. Then the EVM executes the creation bytecode.
The contract address is deterministically calculated using keccak256 hash of RLP encoded sender and nonce - predictable before mining.
g.text: The address is deterministically computed BEFORE the transaction is mined

g.data-narration: This formula means developers can pre-calculate contract addresses - essential for CREATE2 and counterfactual deployments.
After execution, the runtime bytecode is permanently stored at the contract address - this is immutable once deployed.
Lets walk through Alice deploying a Counter contract with specific transaction parameters and resulting address.
g.text: Example: Alice Deploys a Counter Contract

g.data-narration: Key points: empty to field signals deployment, bytecode in data field, deterministic address from sender and nonce.
In summary, contract creation transactions are the foundation for deploying all smart contracts on Ethereum and EVM-compatible chains.
g.text: Deploys smart contracts by sending transaction with empty to and bytecode in data
Address derived from sender and nonce | Code stored permanently | Ready for interactions
